methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage company in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The relevant peer universe comprises one public company (ticker: 277810.KQ) and eight private robotics firms, including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here. While twelve same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the narrow filters—same stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints, falling short of the minimum threshold required for robust triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of robotics valuations across developmental stages and business models, and would not meaningfully constrain the valuation range.

Without a sufficiently sized comparables set, this analysis cannot support a single valuation range using standard multiples-based methods. A defensible valuation for Lumina Vehicles would require either (1) expansion of the peer set through inclusion of adjacent robotics subsectors or earlier venture rounds within the current set, or (2) application of alternative methods such as venture capital method or sum-of-the-parts analysis informed by the firm's technical roadmap and capital efficiency. The key uncertainty remains the ability to identify truly comparable seed-stage robotics manufacturers with published valuation data.
